topic.name: Tax Incentives for Energy and Research
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to tax credits and incentives aimed at promoting energy production, waste management, and research activities.

Document 1: make permanent the tax credit for increasing research activities
increase to 20% the rate of the alternative simplified tax credit for research expenses
Document 2: allow a 30% energy tax credit for investment in qualified waste-to-energy property
establish criteria for awarding certifications for waste-to-energy projects
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2018 the energy tax credit for investment in thermal energy property

topic.name: Medicare Policy Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes aimed at modifying various aspects of Medicare coverage and administration.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to develop an AIM list of medical devices
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to require that the proportion of members of the Medicare Payment Advisory Commission (MEDPAC)
represent the interests of health care providers and Medicare beneficiaries located in rural areas
Document 3: Amends part B (Supplementary Medical Insurance) of title XVIII (Medicare)
limit the penalty for late enrollment under part B to 10% and twice the period of no enrollment

topic.name: Teacher Loan Forgiveness Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing loan forgiveness for teachers through various federal education programs.

Document 1: expands the loan forgiveness available to public elementary and secondary school teachers
Raises to $25,000 the amount of the FFEL or DL outstanding that may be forgiven
Document 2: include a space on the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) form to identify students who are or were in the foster care system
notify students who identify themselves as such of their potential eligibility for financial aid
Document 3: provide up to $17,500 of loan forgiveness under the Federal Family Education Loan and William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan programs
highly qualified elementary and secondary school teachers who are employed in a teacher shortage area

topic.name: U.S. Trade Tariff Amendments
topic.description: Legislation related to amendments of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States concerning duty suspensions and treatments for specific goods.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to provide for the duty-free treatment of certain recreational performance outerwear.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on Acid black 172.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to temporarily extend the suspension of duty on powdered ion exchange resin comprised of a copolymer of styrene, cross linked with divinyl-benzene.

topic.name: Veterans Health Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing medical care and support for veterans and their caregivers.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to provide medical care to a family member of a disabled veteran
Prohibits the caregiver from being subject to deductibles, premiums, copayments, or other fees for such care.
Document 2: Requires the U.S. Postal Service to issue and sell a Veterans Health Care Stamp
to help fund the medical care and treatment of veterans.
Document 3: Prohibits anything in current law provisions authorizing the recovery by the United States of the cost of certain medical care
for care or services furnished to a veteran for a service-connected disability.

topic.name: Oil and Gas Resource Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the exploration, leasing, and environmental protection related to oil and gas resources in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to revise the policy concerning resource development on the outer Continental Shelf
leasing, exploration, or development will be authorized in limited areas only when sound science shows that such activities can proceed with minimal risk to the health of such environments.
Document 2: Authorizes the exploration, leasing, development, production, and economically feasible and prudent transportation of oil and gas in and from the Coastal Plain of Alaska

States that, in connection with specified environmental protection laws, the Secretary shall neither: (1) identify nonleasing alternative courses of action, nor (2) analyze the environmental effects of those actions.
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from authorizing any new lease for exploration or production of oil or natural gas unless the lessee: (1) certifies for each existing lease that the lessee has diligently developed the lands
Establishes a civil penalty for noncompliance with this Act.

topic.name: Immigration Policy and Enforcement
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating immigration, enhancing enforcement, and addressing the status of noncitizens in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to increase employer civil fines for hiring unauthorized aliens.
Sets forth a federal-state allocation of additional penalty funds.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to authorize a DHS official to waive certain entry documentary requirements for nonimmigrant children from Mexico.
Sets forth additional requirements for such entries.
Document 3: Prohibits payment of funds to states unless they compile statistics on arrested persons, including immigration status.
Requires federal agencies to report similar statistics monthly to the FBI.

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons
topic.description: Legislation regarding the accountability and assessment of electromagnetic pulse weapons programs by foreign nations.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Wilderness Area Management
topic.description: Legislation related to the designation, management, and recreational use of wilderness areas in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Wilderness Act to grant members of recreation organizations the right to cross wilderness areas on established trails
Authorizes agencies administering wilderness areas to limit the exercise of such right
Document 2: Designates areas in California as wilderness areas
Components of the National Wilderness Preservation System
Document 3: Designates specified parcels of federal land in central Idaho as wilderness areas
Requires the Secretary of Agriculture to complete a study for establishing the Big Wood Backcountry Trail system

topic.name: Child Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the protection and welfare of children in various contexts, including kinship care, child exploitation, and domestic violence.

Document 1: Amends part E (Foster Care and Adoption Assistance) of title IV of the Social Security Act
eligibility of a child for a kinship guardian assistance payment shall not be affected
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to add child sexual exploitation and child pornography
interception of wire, oral, or electronic communications may be authorized
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to reauthorize, revise, and consolidate grant programs
address domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, and stalking

topic.name: Mental Health and Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on mental health services, research, and community support within public health frameworks.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to authorize appropriations for type I diabetes research and diabetes prevention and treatment through Indian health facilities.
Focus on public health funding for specific health conditions.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to establish programs related to mental health services for children and adolescents.
Involves educational loans and scholarships for mental health professionals.
Document 3: Authorizes funds for mental health services and support for individuals with mental illness.
Involves comprehensive community-based mental health systems.

topic.name: Synthetic Drug Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the control and regulation of synthetic drugs and substances in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Controlled Substances Act
adds certain synthetic opioids, hallucinogens, and cannabinoids to schedule I
Document 2: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
prohibits a person from possessing or receiving unfinished dextromethorphan unless registered

Document 3: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to extend exclusivity for drugs approved for rare diseases

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at managing and restoring water resources and ecosystems in different regions of the United States.

Document 1: Restoring the Everglades, an American Legacy Act of 2007 - Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality.
in accordance with the recommendations of the report of the Chief of Engineers, dated August 6, 2004.
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility and recycled water system.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration, flood control, water quality control, water supply storage, outdoor recreation enhancements, and other aspects of the San Gabriel River Watershed revitalization.

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating mortgage lending practices and protecting homeowners from fraud and foreclosure.

Document 1: amends the Truth in Lending Act
create a safe harbor from lawsuit for creditors
Document 2: Prohibits a foreclosure consultant from receiving compensation
empowers the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the states to enforce this Act
Document 3: establish a mortgage emergency assistance program
Prohibits a mortgagee from accelerating the maturity of or commencing any legal action regarding a mortgage

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection, restoration, and management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000
NOAA must submit a report to Congress describing all activities undertaken to implement the strategy.
Document 2: directs the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy
authorizes the Secretary to make agreements with nonprofit organizations for public-private partnerships promoting coral reef ecosystem conservation.
Document 3: extends the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects
authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function resulting from human activities.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Support
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing support and insurance for agricultural producers against losses and enhancing agricultural programs.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act to establish permanent authority for disaster relief to qualifying agricultural producers.
Provides disaster relief to producers who incur crop or livestock losses due to damaging weather.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act to revise crop insurance provisions regarding approval of advance payments.
Addresses specialty crop research and development costs.
Document 3: Revises agricultural and related programs and provisions respecting producer income, conservation, and rural development.
Covers a broad range of agricultural issues including nutrition, credit, and energy.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at promoting renewable energy and carbon capture technologies.

Document 1: amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002
establish a competitive grant program for renewable and alternative energy fueling infrastructure projects
Document 2: amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program
prohibiting the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from requiring that transportation fuel contain any applicable volume of renewable fuel
Document 3: requires the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to establish a cooperative industry-government research and development program
to demonstrate novel and innovative technologies to capture or prevent carbon dioxide emissions

topic.name: Personal Data Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection and security of personally identifiable information and the requirements for notification in case of data breaches.

Document 1: Requires the Department of Health and Human Services to notify an individual within two business days after discovery of any breach of security.
resulted in the theft or unlawful access of the individual's personally identifiable information.
Document 2: Requires any entity that collects, sells, discloses for consideration, or uses personally identifiable information to provide consumers notice.
implement an information security policy that is applicable to the information security practices and treatment of personally identifiable information.
Document 3: Requires any agency or person that owns or licenses computerized data containing sensitive personal information to develop, implement, and maintain reasonable security and notification procedures.
notify any individual whose sensitive personal information was compromised.

topic.name: Tax Preparation Regulation and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating tax return preparers and enhancing security measures to prevent tax refund fraud.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to direct the Department of the Treasury to establish a program to license or certify and regulate tax return preparers
authorize Treasury to take enforcement action against a tax return preparer for incompetency or wrongdoing.
Document 2: implement security measures in the electronic tax return filing process designed to prevent tax refund fraud from being perpetrated through electronic identity theft

topic.name: Financial Regulatory Reforms
topic.description: Legislative changes aimed at reforming financial institutions and enhancing community investment assessments.

Document 1: Revises the Community Reinvestment Act of 1977
Specifies the community development and other related activities of a regulated financial institution
Document 2: Repeals the authority of the Comptroller General to carry out an onsite examination of an open insured bank
Directs the Comptroller General to complete an audit of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a bank or a depository institution holding company which is an S corporation to have 500 shareholders
Allows the bank or holding company to deduct the amount of such distribution.

topic.name: Wildlife Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at the protection and management of wildlife species and their habitats.

Document 1: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to exempt from such Act the taking of any grizzly bear by an individual in self-defense
reasonable belief of imminent danger posed by the grizzly bear to any individual.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey the McKinney Lake National Fish Hatchery
used by the North Carolina Wildlife Resources Commission as a component of the fish and wildlife management program.
Document 3: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to require the Secretary of the Interior or the Secretary of Commerce to exclude an area from designation as a critical habitat
the benefits of exclusion outweigh the benefits of including the area.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Designations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments and designations related to health, environmental conservation, and federal properties.

Document 1: Women's Health and Cancer Rights Conforming Amendments of 2007
require group health plans to provide coverage for post-mastectomy reconstructive surgery.
Document 2: Designates the U.S. courthouse located at 101 East Pecan Street in Sherman, Texas
as the 'Paul Brown United States Courthouse.'
Document 3: Maine Coastal Islands Wilderness Act of 2011
designates specified lands in Maine within the Cross Island National Wildlife Refuge.

topic.name: Railroad Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing safety practices and technologies in the railroad industry.

Document 1: Short Line Railroad Safety Enhancement Act of 2014
enhance rail safety practices and safety culture.
Document 2: requires each rail carrier that is a Class I railroad
develop and submit for Secretary of Transportation approval a plan to implement a positive train control system.
Document 3: Passenger Train Derailment Prevention Act

require each railroad carrier to submit to the Department of Transportation a list identifying each curve, bridge, tunnel, or other location where the maximum authorized operating speed is at least 20 miles per hour less than the maximum approach speed.

topic.name: STEM and Research Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at promoting education and research in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) fields, including forensic science and nuclear energy.

Document 1: competitive grants for science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) elementary and secondary school programs
support STEM workforce training and career preparation for secondary school students
Document 2: establishes a national forensic science research program to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences
awarding forensic science basic research grants to improve the foundation and practice of forensic science
Document 3: revise the objectives for the civilian nuclear energy research and development programs of the Department of Energy
enhance U.S. capabilities to develop new reactor technologies and related systems technologies

topic.name: Federal Payment Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on preventing improper payments and enhancing the management of federal retirement and benefit systems.

Document 1: Improper Payments Agency Cooperation Enhancement Act of 2013
require the Commissioner of the Social Security Administration to establish and maintain the National Deaths Registry
Document 2: Amends the Employee Retirement Income and Security Act of 1974 to exclude appraisers of employee stock ownership plans from the meaning of a fiduciary
subject to ERISA
Document 3: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 to declare that a qualified preretirement survivor annuity shall not be treated as forfeitable
guarantees the payment by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation of a qualified preretirement survivor annuity

topic.name: Small Business Contracting and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing federal contracting opportunities and support for small businesses, particularly early stage and disadvantaged firms.

Document 1: increased access to federal contract opportunities for early stage small businesses
contracts on the basis of competition restricted to early stage businesses
Document 2: increases the government-wide small business procurement contract goal
SBA must include in its annual report the percentage of participation by small businesses
Document 3: extend through FY2010 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
increase SBIR and STTR set-asides and award levels

topic.name: Indian Water Rights and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the water rights and support for Native American tribes, including settlements, trust management, and related federal assistance.

Document 1: This bill authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Pechanga Settlement Agreement
The bill confirms water rights that must be held in trust by the United States on behalf of the tribe and its allottees.
Document 2: authorizes the Center for Substance Abuse Treatment to award grants to state substance abuse agencies, units of local government, nonprofit organizations, and Indian tribes or tribal organizations
Directs the Attorney General to make grants for medication assisted treatment programs through criminal justice agencies, initiatives involving young people, and recovery services.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, for the purpose of processing acquisitions of real property of the Samish Indian Nation
Prohibits real property taken into trust under this Act from being used for gaming.

topic.name: Social Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at modifying Social Security benefits and related programs for specific populations.

Document 1: Directs the Bureau of Labor Statistics to prepare and publish a monthly index for individuals 62 years of age or older.
Consumer Price Index for Seniors (CPI-S) indicates monthly changes in expenditures.
Document 2: Amends title XVI of the Social Security Act to condition SSI benefits for disabled children on school attendance.
Conditions SSI benefits for disabled children aged 16 or 17 on school attendance.
Document 3: Prohibits the Social Security Administration from issuing a Social Security account number to aliens granted deferred action.
Amends the Social Security Act regarding Social Security account numbers for certain aliens.

topic.name: Regulatory Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing the accountability, analysis, and oversight of federal regulatory processes and costs.

Document 1: Establishes the Office of Regulatory Analysis in the executive branch as an independent establishment.
Requires the Director to publish a regulatory analysis of each proposed covered federal rule.
Document 2: Defines 'major rule' and 'major guidance' for purposes of this Act as a rule or guidance that the Administrator determines is likely to impose significant costs.
Revises procedures for rule making under the Administrative Procedure Act (APA) to require a federal agency to make all preliminary and final factual determinations based on evidence.
Document 3: Requires guidance documents of federal agencies to be considered rules that are subject to the congressional review process.
Significant guidance documents are subject to review as major rules, which delays their effective date.

topic.name: Iran Sanctions and Nuclear Proliferation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at imposing sanctions on Iran to prevent the development of nuclear weapons and address human rights violations.

Document 1: Imposes sanctions with respect to facilitating the extraction or milling of uranium ore in Iran.
Subjects a parent entity... to penalties for violations of certain sanctions regarding Iran.
Document 2: The goal of compelling Iran to abandon efforts to acquire a nuclear weapons capability... can be achieved through a policy that includes economic sanctions.
Amends the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996 to increase from three to five the minimum number of sanctions that the President is required to impose.

topic.name: Housing Assistance Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at providing rental assistance and protections for tenants, particularly in the context of public housing and vulnerable populations.

Document 1: provides 150,000 incremental vouchers to public housing agencies
tenant-based rental housing assistance under section 8 of the United States Housing Act of 1937
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to carry out a rental assistance and supportive housing demonstration program
for the benefit of Indian veterans who are homeless or at-risk of homelessness
Document 3: gives the existing bona fide tenant at least 90-day notice to vacate
requires a housing assistance payment contract to provide that in the case of an owner who is an immediate successor in interest pursuant to foreclosure

topic.name: Federal Regulations and Elections
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to federal property management and election campaign regulations.

Document 1: Federal Real Property Disposal Pilot Program
expedited disposal of real property that is not meeting federal government needs
Document 2: Robocall Privacy Act of 2008
unlawful for a person during specified periods before a federal primary or general election to make certain prerecorded political telephone calls
Document 3: Competitive Elections Act of 2011
prohibit an authorized committee of a candidate for election to the U.S. Senate or the House of Representatives from making any expenditure of funds in support of the candidate's election campaign

topic.name: Consumer Credit Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting consumers from unfair credit practices and managing medical debt reporting.

Document 1: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act to prohibit a consumer reporting agency from making any report containing information related to a fully paid or settled medical debt.
Prohibits construction of this Act as an endorsement by Congress of a 16% APR.
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit the annual percentage rate (APR) applicable to an extension of credit on a credit card account from exceeding 16%.
Declares the intention of Congress that such maximum APR limitation should merely serve as a ceiling on consumer credit cards.
Document 3: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act to exclude from a consumer report information related to a veteran's medical debt.
Provides a mechanism for veterans to dispute the inclusion of program debt already on a credit report.

topic.name: Broadband Access and Spectrum Management
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving broadband access and managing radio spectrum for unlicensed and licensed operations.

Document 1: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to provide additional unlicensed spectrum in the 5850-5925 megahertz band
Directs the FCC to make recommendations to Congress regarding the availability of broadband Internet access using unlicensed spectrum and wireless networks in low income neighborhoods.
Document 2: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to submit to Congress a report on promoting broadband Internet access service for veterans
In preparing the report, the FCC shall provide the public with notice and an opportunity to comment.
Document 3: Amends the National Telecommunications and Information Administration Organization Act to require the Assistant Secretary for Communications and Information at the Department of Commerce and the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to inventory each broadband radio spectrum band of frequencies

Directs the Assistant Secretary and the FCC to submit to Congress a biennial report containing a recommendation of which bands of inventoried frequencies, if any, should be reallocated or made available for shared access.

topic.name: Support for Military Families
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing educational and financial support to military spouses and families of service members.

Document 1: tax credit for qualified relicensing costs for military spouses
amends the Internal Revenue Code
Document 2: transfers educational assistance to surviving spouses
limits assistance to the amount entitled on the date of death
Document 3: ensures educational opportunities for National Guard and Reserves
provides reasonable opportunities to complete educational assignments

topic.name: Consumer Product Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing safety standards and regulations for consumer products to protect public health and safety.

Document 1: Amends the Consumer Product Safety Act to authorize appropriations
Increases from $1.25 million to $20 million the maximum civil penalty
Document 2: Requires every manufacturer of an article for use by a child under 60 months to certify product safety
Bars importation of such articles lacking independent third party certification
Document 3: Directs the Consumer Product Safety Commission to establish a grant program for compliant carbon monoxide alarms
CPSC must report to Congress regarding the implementation of such grant program

topic.name: Labor Relations and Collective Bargaining
topic.description: Legislation aimed at amending the National Labor Relations Act to enhance employee rights and redefine collective bargaining processes.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition regarding collective bargaining representation.
Requires the NLRB to determine a unit as appropriate for collective bargaining if it consists of employees that share a sufficient community of interest.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation to collectively bargain.

Requires the NLRB to decide, before the election of a labor organization as the exclusive collective bargaining representative of all employees of an appropriate unit, whether such unit shall be the employer unit, craft unit, plant unit, or subdivision unit.
Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to deny the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) any power to order an employer to restore or reinstate any work, product, production line, or equipment.
Applies the amendment made by this Act to any complaint for which a final adjudication by the NLRB has not been made by the date of enactment.

topic.name: International Accountability and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at holding foreign states accountable for legal judgments and addressing national security concerns related to enemy combatants and foreign aid restrictions.

Document 1: advocacy within the governing bodies of international organizations for the full compensation and fair treatment of persons in whose favor judgments have been awarded by U.S. courts
protection of economic interests of persons and nations that benefit from a reliable flow of foreign capital by restricting the access to U.S. capital markets of judgment evading foreign states
Document 2: prohibits a U.S. court from ordering the release or parole into the United States of a covered alien
the Secretary of State from issuing any visa to a covered alien that would permit such alien to enter, or be admitted to, the United States
Document 3: prohibits assistance to Brazil until the President certifies to Congress that the government of Brazil has removed the prohibition on extradition of Brazilian nationals to other countries
authorizes the President to waive such prohibition on a case-by-case basis if in the U.S. national interest

topic.name: Educational Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving educational environments and support services for students, including health and safety measures.

Document 1: Student-to-School Nurse Ratio Improvement Act of 2011
reduce such ratio
Document 2: Support for Student Veterans with Families Act
provide child care services on campus for student veterans
Document 3: Amends the Safe and Drug-Free Schools and Communities Act
prevent and respond to incidents of bullying and harassment

topic.name: Election Integrity Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at ensuring fairness and transparency in federal elections through the prevention of deceptive practices and the proper handling of voting technologies and provisional ballots.

Document 1: prohibit any person from knowingly deceiving any other person regarding... the qualifications for or restrictions on voter eligibility
creates a private right of action for any person aggrieved by a violation
Document 2: prohibit a voting system used in a federal election from containing or using any election-dedicated voting system technology which is not deposited by the state
requires the Director of NIST to hold the technology in escrow
Document 3: prohibits any election recount until all provisional votes have been counted
requires a provisional ballot in a federal election to be treated as a voter registration application if it is rejected on the ground that the individual is not registered to vote

topic.name: Firearm Legislation and Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures related to firearms, including regulations on ownership, usage, and protection of rights for veterans and citizens.

Document 1: expands the definition of 'armor piercing ammunition'
prohibits the manufacture, sale, possession, or transfer of certain firearms
Document 2: provides a 90-day amnesty period for veterans to register firearms
grants limited immunity under the federal criminal code
Document 3: declares the right to obtain firearms for self-defense
authorizes legal action for violations of rights under this Act

topic.name: Aviation Regulation and Safety
topic.description: Documents focus on regulations and safety measures related to aviation and air transportation.

Document 1: Revises requirements for the small community air service development program to improve air service to underserved airports.
Treats as eligible airport development activities those related to air service development, including marketing, revenue guarantees, and subsidies.
Document 2: Prohibits the Secretary of Homeland Security from implementing any change to the prohibited items list that would permit passengers to carry small, non-locking knives through passenger screening checkpoints.
Prohibits passengers from carrying certain items on board passenger aircraft.
Document 3: Establishes an Aviation Safety Whistleblower Investigation Office to receive and assess complaints related to aviation safety laws.
Directs the FAA to modify policies to prioritize safety and restrict relationships between FAA employees and air carriers.

topic.name: Food Labeling Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the labeling and regulation of genetically engineered foods and agricultural practices.

Document 1: Genetically Engineered Food Right to Know Act - Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA)
Requires the periodic testing of such foods transferred along a chain of distribution to assure accuracy of labels.
Document 2: Maple-Tapping Access Program Act - Amends the Food Security Act of 1985 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to make grants to states and tribal governments
to encourage owners and operators of privately held farm, ranch, and forest land to voluntarily make such land available for public maple-tapping activities.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments for Education and Environmental Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes aimed at enhancing educational funding for minority-serving institutions and promoting environmental restoration through federal programs.

Document 1: Increases the authorized number of annual appointments to the military service academies
from the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands from one to three
Document 2: amends the Cooperative Forestry Assistance Act of 1978
to establish a landscape-scale restoration program
Document 3: amends titles III and V of the Higher Education Act of 1965
to permit an eligible minority-serving institution of higher education to concurrently receive grant funds

topic.name: Legislative Acts for Protection and Rights
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at protecting the rights and benefits of specific groups, including refugees, veterans, and servicemembers.

Document 1: Indonesian Family Refugee Protection Act
qualifying Indonesian citizen whose asylum claim was denied
Document 2: Veterans Right to Know Act of 2007
notice of the status of such claim
Document 3: Servicemembers Civil Relief Act
6% interest rate limitation on debt during military service

topic.name: Rural Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting and reforming postal services in rural areas of the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the United States Postal Service (USPS) from closing any post office which results in more than 10 miles distance between any 2 post offices.
Grants a right of appeal to the Merit Systems Protection Board to any USPS officer or employer.
Document 2: Imposes a two-year moratorium on the closing or consolidation of any postal facility.
Establishes the Rural Postal Service Advisory Commission to provide strategic guidance on enhancing and protecting postal services in rural areas.
Document 3: Requires the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) to establish a Postal Service Health Benefits Program.
The USPS must establish a Medicare Education Program to notify USPS retirees and employees about the health plans.

topic.name: Immigration and Public Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures related to immigration enforcement, public safety, and the legal consequences associated with criminal activities involving immigrants.

Document 1: Prohibits the use of community oriented policing services funds in contravention of provisions of the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act

restrict a government entity or official from preventing any government entity or official from sending to, or receiving from, the responsible federal immigration agency information regarding an individual's citizenship or immigration status.
Document 2: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to define 'criminal gang.'

Individuals found inadmissible or deportable for criminal gang membership shall be barred from asylum; withholding of removal; temporary protected status; special immigrant juvenile status; deferred action; and parole.
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to make the killing of a Member of Congress or an employee of Congress, or the killing or attempted killing of a law enforcement officer, firefighter, or other first responder, an aggravating factor in death penalty determinations.

topic.name: Abortion Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures related to abortion, including funding restrictions, parental consent requirements, and informed consent protocols.

Document 1: prohibit federal funds from being used to cover any part of the costs of any health plan that includes coverage of abortion services
plans receiving federal funds must keep federal funds segregated from any funds for abortion services
Document 2: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion

Makes it an affirmative defense to a prosecution or civil action under this Act that a defendant reasonably believed that before the minor obtained the abortion, the required parental consent or notification or judicial authorization took place
Document 3: require abortion providers, before a woman gives informed consent to any part of an abortion, to perform an obstetric ultrasound
Prohibits construing this Act to require a woman to view the images or penalizing the physician or the woman if she refuses to look at the images

topic.name: Fire Safety and Disability Integration
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at improving fire safety education and emergency preparedness for individuals with disabilities.

Document 1: establish a program to award grants to institutions of higher education for fire safety education programs
includes educational material specifically prepared for students with physical, sensory, or cognitive disabilities.
Document 2: establish an Office of Disability Integration and Coordination
ensure the integration of people with disabilities into emergency, preparedness, protection, mitigation, evacuation, sheltering, transition, resiliency, and recovery plans.
Document 3: establish a competitive grant program to improve first responder incident response
give priority to a fire service seeking assistance for a fire station located near critical infrastructure.

topic.name: Sexual Assault Prevention Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing and addressing sexual assault within military and veteran contexts.

Document 1: establish and maintain an office within such Chief of Staff on the sexual assault prevention and response program
submit to the congressional defense committees an assessment of the feasibility and advisability of: establishing such an office
Document 2: acquire data, for each calendar year, about sexual offenses, including rape, that occur aboard passenger aircraft
Document 3: develop and implement, by February 1, 2012, a centralized and comprehensive policy on reporting and tracking sexual assaults
submit an annual report to Congress on such incidents and policy implementation

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: This cluster includes various federal legislative acts that address specific rights and governance issues within the United States, including voting rights, judicial appointments, and water resource management.

Document 1: Restores the right of District of Columbia residents to participate as Maryland residents in congressional elections and presidential elections.
Repeals provisions of the District of Columbia Delegate Act.
Document 2: Revises the federal judicial code to remove provisions exempting U.S. attorneys in the Northern Mariana Islands.
Requires each U.S. marshal for the Northern Mariana Islands to reside within such district.
Document 3: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act.
Directs the Secretary to establish at the District a center to provide assistance in the development and advancement of membrane technologies.

topic.name: Currency Valuation and Trade Policy
topic.description: Legislation addressing currency undervaluation and its implications for trade and subsidies in the context of U.S. import policies.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to include as a 'countervailable subsidy'...
Defines 'benefit conferred,' in cases where the currency of a foreign country is exchanged for foreign currency...
Document 2: States that no antidumping duty deposit shall be required upon entry of such a product...
Document 3: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to expand the authority of the administering authority...
Includes exchange-rate misalignment (undervaluation of a foreign currency) by a country as a countervailable subsidy.

topic.name: U.S. Assistance to Haiti
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at providing support and assistance to Haiti in various forms, including reforestation, immigration adjustments for Haitian adoptees, and enhancing global development capacity.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to offer to enter into agreements with the government of Haiti to provide financial assistance, technology transfers, or capacity building assistance to reduce deforestation and increase reforestation in Haiti.
Directs the Secretary to establish a grant program to carry out such activities.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to adjust to permanent resident status an alien who was granted parole admission into the United States pursuant to the humanitarian parole policy for certain Haitian orphans.

Deems such an alien to satisfy the requirements applicable to adopted children if, before the alien is 18 years of age, he or she adjusts to permanent resident status and is adopted by a U.S. citizen.
Document 3: Provides for the hiring of additional Foreign Service officers as United States Agency for International Development (USAID) employees.

topic.name: Family and Employment Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at enhancing family rights and employee benefits in various contexts.

Document 1: Amends part B (Child and Family Services) of title IV of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish and maintain an automated National Responsible Father Registry.
Directs the Secretary to establish a nationwide campaign designed to inform possible fathers, unwed mothers, possible adoptive parents, and eligible parties of the National Registry.
Document 2: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act to require specified employers to provide each eligible employee at least 10 days of paid vacation time during each 12-month period.
The Department of Labor shall conduct a public awareness campaign to inform the public of an eligible employee's entitlement to paid vacation time under the Act.
Document 3: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to provide for employee leave to care for a same-sex spouse, domestic partner, child, parent-in-law, adult child, sibling, grandparent, grandchild, son-in-law, or daughter-in-law.
Amends federal civil service law to apply the same leave allowance to federal employees.

topic.name: Community Development and Economic Empowerment
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing economic opportunities and community development through financial incentives and support for underserved areas.

Document 1: amends the Securities Act of 1933 to exempt qualified 501(c)(3) bonds
allows a tax credit for jurisdictions that forgo taxes
Document 2: facilitate the deployment by the private sector of broadband telecommunications networks
provides a tax credit to holders of qualified technology bonds
Document 3: requires the Secretary of the Treasury to guarantee payments on tax-exempt bonds
supporting revitalization, community stability, or job creation

topic.name: LGBTQ+ Non-Discrimination in Services
topic.description: Legislation aimed at prohibiting discrimination based on sexual orientation and gender identity in adoption, foster care, and housing services.

Document 1: Prohibits an entity that receives federal assistance and is involved in adoption or foster care placements from discriminating against prospective adoptive or foster parents solely on the basis of their sexual orientation, gender identification, or marital status.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to provide specified technical assistance to all entities covered by this Act.
Document 2: Prohibits adoption or foster care placement service entities from using the sexual orientation, gender identity, or marital status of a prospective adoptive or foster parent to deny the opportunity to become an adoptive or foster parent.
Allows individuals aggrieved by a violation of this Act to bring an action seeking relief in federal court.
Document 3: Amends the Fair Housing Act to prohibit discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation or gender identity in housing sales and rentals.
Authorizes appropriations to the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) for educating the public regarding the protections provided by the amendments made by this Act.

topic.name: Commemorative Legislation and Acts
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative acts that establish commemorative commissions or authorize the minting of coins to honor significant historical events or figures in American history.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to mint and issue up to 350,000 $1 coins in commemoration of the centennial of the founding of the Boy Scouts of America.
Requires the coin design to be emblematic of the 100 years of the organization.
Document 2: Establishes the Ronald Reagan Centennial Commission to plan and carry out activities to honor Ronald Reagan on the occasion of the 100th anniversary of his birth.
Prohibits the obligation of federal funds to carry out this Act.

topic.name: Taxation and Regulation of Alcohol and Cannabis
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures related to the taxation and regulation of renewable fuels, distilled spirits, and marijuana enterprises in the United States.

Document 1: require a reduction in the income and excise tax credits for alcohol used for fuel
extend such credits and payments for alcohol fuel mixtures through 2016
Document 2: forfeiture of any amount covered into the treasury with respect to an article containing distilled spirits
determines that either jurisdiction provided an unreasonable subsidy
Document 3: impose an excise tax on the sale of marijuana by producers or importers
requires any person who engages in a marijuana enterprise to obtain a permit

topic.name: U.S. Policy on Vietnam Human Rights
topic.description: Legislation governing U.S. assistance to Vietnam contingent on human rights progress.

Document 1: Prohibits U.S. nonhumanitarian assistance to the government of Vietnam in excess of FY2012 amounts unless the President certifies to Congress that the government of Vietnam has made substantial progress respecting political, media, and religious freedoms.
Expresses the sense of Congress that the United States should take measures to overcome the jamming of Radio Free Asia by Vietnam.
Document 2: Prohibits the federal government from providing the government of Vietnam with nonhumanitarian development, trade, economic, and security assistance during any fiscal year that exceeds the amount of such assistance provided during FY2008, unless such increases are matched or exceeded by additional funding for programs focusing on human rights and democracy promotion.
Declares it is U.S. policy that educational and cultural exchange programs with Vietnam should promote progress toward freedom and democracy.

topic.name: Tick-Borne Disease Management
topic.description: Legislative efforts focused on the prevention, education, and research related to Lyme and other tick-borne diseases.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish the Tick-Borne Diseases Advisory Committee.
Requires the Secretary to provide for the coordination of all federal programs and activities related to Lyme and other tick-borne diseases.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish the Tick-Borne Diseases Advisory Committee.
Requires the Secretary to provide for the coordination of all federal programs and activities related to Lyme and other tick-borne diseases.
Document 3: Includes within authorized preventive health services available to veterans through the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) immunizations against infectious diseases.

topic.name: Tax and Revenue Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to taxation, revenue generation, and financial regulations in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Mineral Leasing Act to repeal the requirement that the Department of the Interior offer under a deferred bonus lease payment system
Interior must make publicly available appraisal reports, individual and total lease sales, high bids, royalty payments, and related revenues
Document 2: authorizes each member state under the Streamlined Sales and Use Tax Agreement to require all sellers to collect and remit sales and use taxes
extends the ban on state and local taxation of Internet access and on multiple or discriminatory taxes on electronic commerce
Document 3: makes the repeal of the estate and generation-skipping transfer taxes under the Economic Growth and Tax Reconciliation Act of 2001 permanent
the taxes would be reinstated after December 31, 2010

topic.name: Mine Safety and Health Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving safety standards and health regulations in mining operations.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977
Requires the Secretary of Labor to conduct health and safety related accident investigations in coal or other mines.
Document 2: Establishes in the Treasury the Mine Safety Grant Fund
Directs the Secretary of Labor to award competitive Brookwood-Sago Mine Safety Grants to entities for research and outreach to prevent and treat Black Lung Disease.

topic.name: Unemployment Compensation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the extension and modification of unemployment compensation programs in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until five months after enactment of this Act requirements that federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.
Document 2: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA)
Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program through November 30, 2010.

topic.name: Gambling Regulation and Awareness
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation, prevention, and awareness of problem gambling and related activities.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to carry out a national campaign to increase knowledge and raise awareness of problem gambling.
Authorizes the Secretary to make grants to states, local governments, and nonprofit agencies to provide comprehensive services with respect to treatment and prevention of problem gambling.
Document 2: Prohibits entering a horse in a race that is subject to an interstate off-track wager if the person knows the horse is under the influence of a performance-enhancing drug.
Sets forth penalties for violations, including civil penalties and suspension of a person providing a horse with such drug.

topic.name: Military and Government Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to military service, funding for veterans and their families, and measures to ensure government transparency and accountability.

Document 1: Children of Fallen Soldiers Pell Grant Act of 2007
eliminate the expected family contribution used in determining Pell Grant eligibility
Document 2: No More Ghost Money Act

Prohibits any federal department, agency, or federal government contractor from making a monetary payment to any official of a foreign country for the purposes of bribery, coercion, or any illegal activity

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Trade
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to U.S. foreign relations, particularly concerning Cuba and foreign medical education.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to eliminate the exemption of certain foreign medical schools...
Preserves the Direct Loan eligibility of students who were enrolled at schools...
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to repeal the embargo placed upon all trade with Cuba.
Permits installation and maintenance of telecommunications equipment and facilities in Cuba.
Document 3: Prohibits the President from regulating or prohibiting travel to or from Cuba by U.S. citizens or legal residents...
States that such provision does not restrict presidential authority in time of war or armed hostilities.

topic.name: U.S. National Security and Immigration Policy
topic.description: Legislation addressing national security concerns related to citizenship, identification, and non-citizen verification processes.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include grounds for loss of U.S. nationality by citizens involved with foreign terrorist organizations.
Prohibits the Secretary of State from issuing passports to individuals engaged in hostilities against the U.S.
Document 2: Authorizes financial institutions to verify identity of non-U.S. citizens using limited forms of identification.
Prohibits acceptance of foreign government-issued identification for account opening.

topic.name: Mining and Reclamation Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, regulation, and reclamation of mining activities and associated infrastructure.

Document 1: Bureau of Reclamation's efforts to maintain in a reliable manner all reserved works
standardize and streamline data reporting and processes across regions
Document 2: repeal the $15 million limitation placed upon the total annual payment to certified states or Indian tribes in connection with abandoned mine reclamation
Document 3: Prohibits the issuance of a federal patent for any mining claim
Subjects production of locatable minerals from any mining claim to a royalty of between 2 % and 5% of the value of the production

topic.name: Worker Health and Compensation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving health and compensation benefits for workers affected by occupational diseases and conditions.

Document 1: Amends the Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program Act of 2000
Requires the President to establish an Advisory Board on Toxic Substances and Worker Health.
Document 2: Amends the Black Lung Benefits Act to revise requirements with regard to miners' claims for pneumoconiosis
Establishes an irrebuttable presumption that a miner is totally disabled due to black lung disease.
Document 3: Amends the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1993 regarding waivers of small miner claim maintenance fees
Extends the allowance of a certain period to cure a defect in the waiver application.

topic.name: Child Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving child protection through criminal background checks and related measures.

Document 2: Amends the National Child Protection Act of 1993 to direct the Attorney General to establish policies and procedures to streamline the process of obtaining state and national criminal history background checks on covered individuals.
Defines 'covered entity' as any business or organization that provides care to children.
Document 3: Amends the National Child Protection Act of 1993 to direct the Attorney General to establish policies and procedures for a program for national criminal history background checks for child-serving organizations.
Limits the liability of a child-serving organization for failure to conduct criminal background checks.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative acts that focus on specific regulatory changes and enforcement mechanisms across different sectors.

Document 1: Disposal of Excess Federal Lands Act of 2015 directs the Secretary of the Interior to offer for disposal by competitive sale certain federal lands.
previously identified as suitable for disposal in the report submitted to Congress.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to replace the Federal Election Commission with the Federal Election Administration.
Authorizes the FEA to impose civil penalties, issue cease-and-desist orders, and report apparent criminal violations.
Document 3: This bill amends the federal judicial code to prohibit federal courts from certifying any proposed class seeking monetary relief for personal injury or economic loss.
Such a trust shall file with the bankruptcy court quarterly reports, available on the public docket.

topic.name: Veterans and Economic Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at providing financial relief and support to veterans and their families, as well as promoting economic self-sufficiency for individuals in need.

Document 1: allow veterans a deduction from gross income for their travel expenses
treatment related to a service-connected disability
Document 2: provide individuals and families of limited means with an incentive to save a portion of their earned income
increase their economic self-sufficiency
Document 3: expand the allowable uses of Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund amounts
payment of costs to carry out environmental assessment studies

topic.name: Military Personnel Relocation and Immigration
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the relocation of military personnel and the immigration status of religious workers in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to extend the special immigrant religious worker program through October 1, 2013.
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make the special immigrant non-minister religious worker program permanent.
Document 2: Establishes the Guam Defense Policy Review Initiative Account to carry out transactions in connection with the realignment and relocation of military personnel on Guam.
Requires the Secretary of Defense to develop and support programs for training the local workforce on Guam for employment opportunities arising from realignment and relocation.

topic.name: Climate Change and Security
topic.description: Legislation addressing the implications of climate change on national security and the need for research and response measures.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to submit to Congress a National Intelligence Estimate on the anticipated geopolitical effects of global climate change
Requires the Secretary of Defense to report to Congress on the projected impact on military installations, capabilities, and operations of the effects of global climate change.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to establish a program of extramural awards to conduct scientific research on abrupt climate change.

topic.name: Support for Vulnerable Populations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative efforts aimed at providing support and resources to vulnerable populations, including students and veterans, through various programs and grants.

Document 3: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish a program of awarding grants to states to assist veterans who received and completed military emergency medical training
provide reimbursement for costs associated with such course work and training and with applying for licensure or certification.

topic.name: Federal Land and Education Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to land management, educational funding, and environmental remediation in the United States.

Document 1: directs the Department of the Interior to conduct sales of eligible lands in Mohave County, Arizona
Each sale of eligible land shall be through a competitive bidding process
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to make the formula for calculation of an individual Pell Grant inapplicable for the 2007-2008 academic year
suspends for such year the 'tuition sensitivity provision'
Document 3: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to authorize appropriations for the remediation of sediment contamination
research on the development and use of innovative approaches, technologies, and techniques for the remediation of sediment contamination

topic.name: Regulatory Framework for Financial Lending
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating various aspects of lending practices, particularly for community lenders and specific borrower groups.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act to exempt loans made to nonprofit religious organizations from limitations on the volume of loans.
Focuses on lending practices related to religious organizations.
Document 2: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act to prohibit the CFPB from auditing responsible community mortgage lenders without specific requests.
Addresses regulations affecting community mortgage lenders and their operational constraints.
Document 3: Requires creditors to provide specific disclosures to members of the Armed Forces regarding consumer credit extensions.
Focuses on transparency and protection for military personnel in lending practices.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts pertaining to tax credits, military resources, and federal income tax provisions.

Document 1: Makes permanent the increases in the child tax credit enacted by the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001.
Provides for an annual inflation adjustment to child tax credit amounts.
Document 2: Authorizes the U.S. Coast Guard to renovate and reactivate the Coast Guard Cutter Polar Sea.
Requires the Government Accountability Office to submit to Congress a report on international funding models for icebreaking fleets.
Document 3: Excludes from gross income any payments made by Virginia Tech to the victims of the tragic shootings.
Increases the penalty for failure to file a partnership income tax return.

topic.name: Veterans and Military Affairs
topic.description: Legislation related to the rights, recognition, and support of military personnel and veterans in the United States.

Document 1: Fair Military Leave Act
payment to each member of the Armed Forces
Document 3: Veterans Day Moment of Silence Act
honor of the service and sacrifice of veterans

topic.name: Insurance Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: The documents focus on the regulation and oversight of insurance practices and the establishment of federal frameworks for insurance operations.

Document 1: Establishes in the Department of the Treasury an Office of National Insurance (ONI)
Requires national insurers to obtain a federal license.
Document 2: Directs the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to research how commercially available small and medium sized unmanned aircraft...
Authorize DHS to provide the Departments of Defense, Transportation, and Energy...
Document 3: Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to reestablish the National Association of Registered Agents and Brokers as a nonprofit corporation
Sets limits upon permissible uses of such criminal background information.

topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislative measures concerning the exploration, development, and production of oil and gas resources in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the President to review all projects for the exploration, development, or production of oil and gas resources under federal leases.
Shields decisions of the President from further administrative or judicial review.
Document 2: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease for oil or natural gas off the coast of California, Oregon, or Washington.
Document 3: Bars the Secretary of the Interior from finalizing or implementing provisions regarding oil and gas well stimulation without a study.
Requires the Secretary to submit a preliminary and final report to Congress on the results of such study.

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving child welfare, safety, and health through various programs and initiatives.

Document 1: Family Cord Blood Banking Act
cost of private umbilical cord blood or tissue banking services as a medical care expense
Document 2: Childcare Accountability and Responsibility Act
national criminal background check for employees of child care providers
Document 3: Gulf of Mexico Restoration and Protection Act
coordinate actions of EPA and federal agencies for restoration plans

topic.name: Consumer Protection in Health and Fraud
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting consumers, particularly vulnerable populations like seniors, through improved access to health information and fraud prevention measures.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to utilize an Internet site to consolidate and translate health care information.
Establish programs to gather information from health care practitioners and make such information publicly available regarding off-label uses of new drugs.
Document 2: Directs the Federal Trade Commission to establish an office to advise on the prevention of fraud targeting seniors.
Disseminate information on the most common fraud schemes targeting seniors.

topic.name: Education and Manufacturing Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to education funding and manufacturing standards in the United States.

Document 1: Universal College Credit Act - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit up to $5,000 annually for the qualified tuition and related expenses.
Allows such credit for four years of undergraduate education expenses and six years of graduate or professional education expenses.
Document 2: Universal College Credit Act - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit up to $5,000 annually for the qualified tuition and related expenses.
Excludes expenses for courses involving sports, games, or hobbies (unless part of a degree program).
Document 3: Made in America Act of 2013 - Directs the Secretary of Commerce to establish a voluntary America Star Program.
Requires the labels to be consistent with public perceptions of the meaning of descriptions of the extent to which a product is manufactured in the United States.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing consumer rights and protections across various sectors including telecommunications, financial literacy, and health.

Document 1: Requires the FCC to direct providers to permit subscribers to unlock any type of wireless device.
Prohibits this Act from being construed to alter the terms of any valid contract between a provider and a subscriber.
Document 2: Authorizes the Director of the CFPB to make competitive grants to support financial literacy education for young people and families.
Defines 'eligible institution' as a partnership of educational and financial entities.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require mammography facilities to include information on breast density in reports.
Requires communication of risks associated with breast density and recommendations for supplemental screening.

topic.name: Health and Technology Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on health care reform and technological advancements in health and environmental applications.

Document 1: establish a program to award prizes to eligible persons for achievement in one or more applications of nanotechnology
improvement of human health, consistent with regulations promulgated by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA)
Document 2: direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to issue guidelines
provide for the establishment of a permanent pathway to enable individuals to enroll in qualified health plans
Document 3: Repeals the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for qualified health insurance costs

topic.name: Government Legislation on Education and Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at regulating educational data reporting and enhancing benefits for veterans and students.

Document 1: Delays the implementation, administration, or enforcement of the standards for mercury and hazardous air pollutants emitted from new power plants.
Lower Electric Bill Act of 2014.
Document 2: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require institutions of higher education to submit data.
Directs the Secretary to require IHEs to identify student unit records.

topic.name: Legislation on Rights and Protections
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at defining and protecting specific rights and freedoms within the United States.

Document 1: Limits the remedies of an injured party in a civil action for deprivation of rights to injunctive and declaratory relief
Prohibits the award of attorney's fees to a prevailing party in any action that is brought against the United States
Document 2: Deems human life to exist from conception
Requires that the term 'person' include all such human life
Document 3: Makes it unlawful for any person in the United States to cause any caller identification service to knowingly transmit misleading or inaccurate caller ID information
Provides civil and criminal penalties for violations

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts that amend existing laws to enhance community services, surveillance regulations, and water management practices.

Document 1: Amends the 21st Century Community Learning Centers program under title IV of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
Raises from 3% to 5% the percentage of program funds a state may use for monitoring, evaluating, and providing training and technical assistance to community learning center grantees.
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 to require the FBI to include a statement of specific and articulable facts
Removes a requirement that a judge considering a petition to modify or set aside a nondisclosure order treat as conclusive a certification by the Attorney General.
Document 3: Considers voluntary water transfers to meet specified conditions for authorized transfers of Central Valley Project water
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to initiate and complete on an expedited basis the programmatic development of environmental documentation to facilitate voluntary water transfers.

topic.name: Marine Resource Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the assessment and management of marine resources, including minerals and fisheries, in U.S. waters.

Document 1: conduct and report to Congress on an assessment of all available domestic technological capabilities required for the location and the efficient and environmentally sound recovery of minerals
a survey of the shallow and deep seabed of the United States to identify sites for the recovery of such minerals
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to conduct a benchmark stock assessment of the South Atlantic red snapper fishery and report to Congress

Prohibits the Secretary from promulgating any interim rule under specified provisions of the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act that prohibits fishing in the South Atlantic red snapper fishery before such report demonstrates the need for interim or final rules
Document 3: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to provide that a Regional Fishery Management Council is not required to develop an annual catch limit for an ecosystem component species
Requires each annual catch limit to take into account management measures under international agreements in which the United States participates

topic.name: Legal Procedures and Recording
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing transparency and accountability in legal proceedings through recording and training initiatives.

Document 1: Custodial Interrogation Recording Act
complete and accurate recording, by both audio and video means, of every custodial interrogation
Document 2: grant to a nonprofit organization for a national training program
improve the professional skills of state and local prosecutors
Document 3: Sunshine in the Courtroom Act of 2009
photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings

topic.name: Legislative Acts for Public Welfare
topic.description: A collection of legislative acts aimed at enhancing public welfare through various means including immigration, environmental protection, and housing stabilization.

Document 1: E-2 (treaty investor) nonimmigrant status
Allows aliens having E-2 nonimmigrant status to re-enter the United States without requesting a new visa.
Document 2: Requires public buildings to incorporate bird-safe materials
Addresses impacts of lighting on native bird species.
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for redevelopment of abandoned homes
Requires allocation of funds to areas with the greatest need.

topic.name: Veterans and Environmental Health Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to veterans' benefits, ecological restoration initiatives, and access to healthcare services for underserved populations.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to treat as a dependent child any unmarried person who meets specific criteria related to veterans' care expenses.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to establish a Collaborative Forest Landscape Restoration Program to fund ecological restoration treatments for priority forest landscapes.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to establish a Frontline Providers Loan Repayment Program for health professionals providing services in scarcity areas.

topic.name: Military and Environmental Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at supporting military families and regulating environmental impacts related to fossil fuels and oil spills.

Document 3: End Polluter Welfare Act of 2015 - Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act and the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to repeal the authority of the Department of the Interior to reduce or eliminate royalty payments for oil and natural gas leases in the Outer Continental Shelf.
Amends the Mineral Leasing Act to increase minimum royalty payments for coal, oil, and natural gas leases.

topic.name: Maritime Transportation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation and taxation of maritime transportation and port operations in the United States.

Document 1: Clean Ports Act of 2011 - federal preemption of state and local law relating to motor carriers at port facilities.
